Clusters.Interpretation: GPU driver reset, TDR, or kernel graphics path failure.
Clusters.NextAction: Check GPU driver, overlays, HAGS/MPO/capture features, thermals, power, and WPR/GPU evidence.

Clusters.Interpretation: The process accessed invalid memory. Common causes include injected overlays/hooks, graphics/runtime bugs, corrupt data, or driver interactions.
Clusters.NextAction: Capture a dump, isolate overlays/hooks, test clean boot, repair runtimes, and compare the fault module.

Signals.Action: Treat this as system-wide until proven otherwise: compare GPU/TDR, drivers, runtimes, security, storage, memory, and Windows health before app-specific fixes.
Prioritize overlay/hook isolation, runtime repair, clean boot, and dump capture before repeating data wipes.
Use DxDiag/WPR, disable capture overlays, verify power/thermal state, and test a clean GPU driver path.

Signals.Evidence: 3 recent driver/update change(s) near latest crash.
Signals.Action: Isolate one update/driver variable at a time; avoid stacking fixes in one test.
Install/use missing Microsoft tools only if escalation evidence is needed.
